expectations.evidence: Abstract opens with 'Accurate digital terrain models (DTMs) derived from airborne laser scanning are essential...' — no filler opener present.
First use reads: 'Actueel Hoogtebestand Nederland 4 (AHN4) dataset' — fully expanded with abbreviation in parentheses.

Gap is stated as: 'existing ground-filtering methods degrade in performance over complex urban scenes and dense vegetation without auxiliary inputs such as full-waveform data or manually tuned parameters' — names concrete limitations (auxiliary inputs, parameter tuning, dense urban canopy).

No instance of 'good performance'. Replaced with: 'achieves a root mean square error (RMSE) of X.XX m and an mean absolute error (MAE) of X.XX m, outperforming the classical ground-filtering baseline by XX%' — uses quantitative placeholders as specified acceptable.

Problem: DTMs essential for flood risk assessment, urban planning, hydrological modelling. Gap: existing methods degrade without auxiliary inputs. Method: Residual U-Net trained end-to-end on DSMs. Result: RMSE X.XX m, MAE X.XX m, XX% over baseline. Significance: deep learning replaces labour-intensive parameter tuning, implications for national mapping pipelines.

All five sentences are medium-to-long (approximately 25-50 words each); no short punchy sentence appears among them. Sentences 2 and 3 share a similar scaffold ('[Subject] [verb phrase], [participial/relative clause]'): 'We propose a Residual U-Net..., requiring no additional...' and 'The model was trained and evaluated..., which provides...' are consecutive and structurally parallel. The abstract lacks the required variety in sentence length and rhythm.
Word count is approximately 155 words — well under the 200-word limit.
